description: High-energy EDM track with euphoric builds, massive drops, and minimal but impactful vocals
suno_tags: progressive house, euphoric EDM, big drop, synth leads, four-on-the-floor, festival energy, 128 BPM
vocal_style: Ethereal and atmospheric in verses — like a voice floating above the beat. Minimal words, maximum emotion. Breathy, reverb-drenched, gender-neutral. Vocals disappear during drops to let the synths take over.

tips: Less is more — EDM vocals are texture, not storytelling
Use short, atmospheric phrases that create a feeling, not a narrative
Build sections should have ascending energy in the words
Drop sections are instrumental — mark them clearly with [Drop] tag
Breakdown is the emotional breathing room between drops

example_lyrics:
[Intro]
[Instrumental]
[Analog Synth]

[Verse]
[Breathy]
Close your eyes and let go
We're floating in the afterglow

[Pre-Chorus]
[Energy: Building]
Higher, higher
Take me to the light
Higher, higher
Into the night

[Drop]
[Instrumental]

[Break]
[Soft]
Breathe

[Verse]
[Breathy]
Feel the pulse beneath your skin
Let the waves pull you in

[Pre-Chorus]
[Energy: Building]
Higher, higher
Take me to the light
Higher, higher
We're alive tonight

[Drop]
[Instrumental]

[Outro]
[Soft]
We're alive tonight
[Effect: Fade Out]
[End]
do: Use [Build] and [Drop] tags — they're essential for EDM structure
Keep total lyrics under 20 lines — the production carries the song
Make build sections feel like ascending stairs — each line higher energy
Include a breakdown section with minimal sound for contrast
Use breathy, reverb-heavy vocal delivery tags
dont: Don't write long verses — 2-4 lines maximum per vocal section
Don't add lyrics during the drop — it's purely instrumental energy
Don't tell a complex story — EDM is about sensation, not narrative
Don't use acoustic instrument tags — this is electronic territory
Don't forget the build-drop dynamic — it's the entire point of the genre
